content.problemStatement:
This problem is worth 50 points.

Let AAA denote a permutation of length NNN.

Define f(A)=∑i=1Nmin⁡(Ai,Ai+1)f(A) = \sum_{i = 1}^{N} \min(A_i, A_{i + 1})f(A)=∑i=1N​min(Ai​,Ai+1​), where AN+1=A1A_{N + 1} = A_1AN+1​=A1​.

Find the **minimum** value of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all permutations of length NNN.

Note that a permutation of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input containing NNN, the size of the permutation.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the minimum value of f(A)f(A)f(A).


**Note** : The answer may not fit in a 32-bit integer. You are recommended to use 64-bit integers.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (3 ≤ N ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimum possible value of f(A) for a permutation of length N.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=3 (odd) k=1, answer = (1+1)^2 = 4. For N=4 (even) k=2, answer = 2*3 = 6.

solution.approach: In a cycle each element has two neighbours, therefore an element can be the smaller endpoint of at most two edges. To minimise the sum we let the smallest numbers be the minimum of as many edges as possible. For N = 2k (even) the numbers 1..k each serve as the minimum for two edges, giving sum = 2·(1+2+…+k) = k·(k+1). For N = 2k+1 (odd) the numbers 1..k cover 2k edges and the next smallest number (k+1) covers the remaining single edge, giving sum = 2·(1+2+…+k) + (k+1) = (k+1)². The answer can be computed in O(1) per test case using 64‑bit integers.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.
